article:
In brief: we're expecting a mostly dry and hot week ahead in Abuja, with temperatures soaring into the mid-30s by the weekend. However, by next Monday and Tuesday, we could see a significant increase in rain chances, which may bring some relief from the heat. Let's dive into the details of what you can expect this week.

As we start the day, it's already feeling quite warm and muggy, with the temperature at 25°C, but feeling more like 31°C due to the high humidity of 94%. The wind is barely noticeable, blowing at a gentle 3 km/h, which isn't doing much to help with the heat. If you're planning on spending time outdoors today, be sure to stay hydrated and take breaks in the shade, especially if you're walking around the National Assembly Complex or visiting the Abuja National Mosque. 

The UV Index is currently at 0, which is relatively low, but don't let that fool you - the sun's rays can still be strong, especially during the peak hours of the day. As we head into the rest of the week, we're expecting plenty of sunshine, which will contribute to the rising temperatures. If you're planning on driving along the Nnamdi Azikiwe Expressway or exploring the city, be prepared for the heat and try to avoid traveling during the hottest part of the day.

### Thursday, April 10
Today is expected to be a dry and hot day, with a high of 33°C and a low of 25°C. The rain chance is at 0%, so you can leave your umbrella at home. It'll be a great day to get outdoors and enjoy the city, whether that's visiting the Jabi Lake or taking a stroll around the Millennium Park. Just be sure to stay hydrated and take breaks in the shade, especially during the peak sun hours. [MAP: Current weather conditions in Abuja]

As we head into the afternoon, the temperature will continue to rise, and the heat index will make it feel even hotter. If you're planning on attending any outdoor events or activities, be prepared for the heat and try to stay cool. The good news is that the humidity will remain relatively high, which may help to keep the temperature from rising too much. However, it's still going to feel quite hot, so be sure to take care of yourself and stay safe.

### Friday, April 11
Tomorrow is looking similar to today, with a high of 35°C and a low of 24°C. The rain chance is still very low, at just 3%, so it's unlikely we'll see any precipitation. It'll be another great day to get outdoors and enjoy the city, whether that's visiting the Abuja National Park or taking a walk around the city. Just be sure to stay hydrated and take breaks in the shade, especially during the peak sun hours. The heat index will continue to be a factor, making it feel even hotter than the actual temperature.

As we head into the weekend, the temperatures are expected to remain hot, with highs in the mid-30s and lows in the mid-20s. It'll be a great time to enjoy the city's outdoor spaces, such as the Unity Fountain or the National Children's Park. Just be sure to take care of yourself and stay safe in the heat. The humidity will continue to be relatively high, which may help to keep the temperature from rising too much. However, it's still going to feel quite hot, so be sure to stay hydrated and take breaks in the shade.

### Weekend Outlook
The weekend is looking hot and dry, with plenty of sunshine expected. The temperatures will continue to soar, with highs in the mid-30s and lows in the mid-20s. It'll be a great time to enjoy the city's outdoor spaces, such as the Jabi Lake or the Millennium Park. Just be sure to take care of yourself and stay safe in the heat. The heat index will continue to be a factor, making it feel even hotter than the actual temperature. [MAP: 7-day forecast for Abuja]

As we head into next week, the rain chances start to increase, which may bring some relief from the heat. By Monday and Tuesday, we're expecting a significant increase in rain chances, with a 55% chance of precipitation on Tuesday. This could bring some much-needed relief from the heat, and may even help to lower the temperatures a bit. However, it's still too early to say for sure, and we'll need to keep an eye on the forecast as we get closer to the weekend.

### Monday, April 14
By Monday, the rain chances start to increase, with a 35% chance of precipitation. The high temperature will be around 36°C, with a low of 26°C. It'll be a good idea to check the forecast regularly, as the rain chances may change throughout the day. If you're planning on attending any outdoor events or activities, be prepared for the possibility of rain and make sure to have a backup plan in case the weather changes.

### Tuesday, April 15
On Tuesday, the rain chances increase even more, with a 55% chance of precipitation. The high temperature will be around 34°C, with a low of 26°C. It'll be a good idea to check the forecast regularly, as the rain chances may change throughout the day. If you're planning on attending any outdoor events or activities, be prepared for the possibility of rain and make sure to have a backup plan in case the weather changes. [MAP: Satellite imagery of the region]

As we head into the rest of the week, the rain chances will continue to be a factor, with a 46% chance of precipitation on Wednesday. The temperatures will start to decrease, with highs in the mid-30s and lows in the mid-20s. It'll be a good idea to stay tuned to the forecast, as the weather may change throughout the week. We'll be keeping a close eye on the situation and will provide updates as necessary. For now, it's looking like a hot and dry week ahead, with a chance of rain by next Monday and Tuesday. Stay cool and stay safe, Abuja! [MAP: Current weather conditions in Abuja and surrounding areas]
